issues.title: Are we inline with the object security draft?
issues.author: zaheduzzaman
issues.body:
https://github.com/gloinul/draft-westerlund-moq-overview/blob/c9f8857138fdfb5bd39c816b1cfd80b258644627/draft-westerlund-moq-overview.md?plain=1#L510-L514

In practice the non-modifiability depends on the mechanism being used for protection, hence we can say some properties are intended to remain unchanged across relays, subscribes need to verify such properties are not changed in transit.

issues.title: mentioning of sender side adaptation
issues.author: zaheduzzaman
issues.body:
https://github.com/gloinul/draft-westerlund-moq-overview/blob/c9f8857138fdfb5bd39c816b1cfd80b258644627/draft-westerlund-moq-overview.md?plain=1#L606

In future we would also need to include the possibility of sender side adaptation.

issues.title: Is blindly caching allowed?
issues.author: zaheduzzaman
issues.body:
https://github.com/gloinul/draft-westerlund-moq-overview/blob/c9f8857138fdfb5bd39c816b1cfd80b258644627/draft-westerlund-moq-overview.md?plain=1#L529-L530

While this is true, the relay still need to apply authorization and publisher trust policy before caching - this hinted in the dos draft. May be we should clarify this.

issues.title: Session continuation after migration depends on other factors too
issues.author: zaheduzzaman
issues.body:
https://github.com/gloinul/draft-westerlund-moq-overview/blob/c9f8857138fdfb5bd39c816b1cfd80b258644627/draft-westerlund-moq-overview.md?plain=1#L658-L660

GOAWAY supports graceful migration, but continuity still depends on implementation, buffering, object retention, authorization continuity, and timing. The transport draft includes GOAWAY/session management mechanisms, but does not magically guarantee zero disruption in all deployments. Hence it would be good to highlight this in the overview document.

Suggested text 

`GOAWAY supports graceful session migration and can reduce disruption during relay maintenance or replacement, but continuity depends on object availability, timing, and application/session policy`

issues.title: Subgroup definition
issues.author: zaheduzzaman
issues.body:
https://github.com/gloinul/draft-westerlund-moq-overview/blob/c9f8857138fdfb5bd39c816b1cfd80b258644627/draft-westerlund-moq-overview.md?plain=1#L458

we have two definition of subgroups in the draft. Also does this description covers the case when Forwarding preference is DATAGRAM? if not we may mention that this description is only allowed for streaming mood.

pulls.title: added sections to cover the publisher <--> relay <--> subscriber interaction
pulls.author: zaheduzzaman
pulls.body: The idea is to describe how everything works togather.

comments.author: gloinul
comments.body: I think these are interesting questions and good to clarify. But I am expecting the initial answer that this is proprietary and that third party relays will not really work.

comments.author: zaheduzzaman
comments.body:
> I think these are interesting questions and good to clarify. But I am expecting the initial answer that this is proprietary and that third party relays will not really work.

Right, this is why I would say that it is important to describe what is possible and not possible with current MoQT. This would then help to either scoping the relay usage or design improvement to allow more seemless integration of 3rd party relays.
